concept_desc: A claim lacks proper attribution, either crediting the wrong source or presenting information as fact without citation.
A claim includes swapped, incorrectly specified, or inserted noun phrases (e.g. one named entity used in a context where another word is expected).
A claim has a different number than the original context (e.g. 20% vs. 0.7%). Any number, including year, dimensions, ages, etc.
A claim is based on accurate contextual information but is too broad or too general to be supported by the context.
A claim does not accurately incorporate tense, modality (e.g. might vs. will), or time reference in relation to the context.
